title: Functions 3
description: Functions can return values in Rust. The return type is declared with an arrow `->` after the parameter list.
instructions: Fix the function so it correctly returns a value.
hints: In Rust, the last expression in a function is automatically returned.
Note the difference: `num * num;` (with semicolon) is a statement, not an expression.
Remove the semicolon to make it an expression that gets returned, or use `return num * num;`.
explanation: In Rust, the last expression in a function body is implicitly returned. Adding a semicolon turns an expression into a statement, which returns `()` (the unit type) instead. This is a common source of confusion for newcomers. You can also use the `return` keyword for early returns.
